forager-smart.description: Exception route for implementation where the base worker is likely to make a design mistake. Use only for ambiguous behavior, cross-file state changes, or edge cases requiring a choice between competing implementations. Default to forager-worker for normal edits, straightforward fixes, and bounded feature work.

forager-capable.description: Exception route for implementation that depends on heavy local execution. Use only when the task requires broad edits plus running or debugging checks, generated artifacts, or multi-step tool workflows. Default to forager-worker when the files and verification path are already clear. Do not use for strategy, review, or research.

forager-documents.description: Exception route for prose-dominant work. Use only when the main deliverable is documentation, operator guidance, specs, reports, or substantial human-facing text. Default to forager-worker when documentation is incidental to code changes. Do not use for code review.

forager-simple.description: Exception route for small mechanical implementation. Use only when the files, edit shape, and verification path are obvious: renames, config tweaks, simple fixes, or bounded edits. Default to forager-worker for normal task-backed implementation. Do not use for architecture-sensitive, ambiguous, or multi-system changes.

forager-ui.description: Exception route for UI implementation. Use only when the main change affects user-visible interface behavior, layout, styling, accessibility, React/component state, or UX polish. Default to forager-worker for backend work or incidental UI text edits. Do not use for review-only tasks.

plan-reviewer-xhigh-reasoning.description: Escalation-only xhigh-reasoning plan reviewer. Use only when the default plan-reviewer cannot resolve a concrete blocker involving cross-system sequencing, irreversible migration risk, conflicting requirements, or worker handoff likely to fail. Default to plan-reviewer for routine plan review, missing detail, and general quality checks. Returns plan blockers, not architecture advice or code review.

code-reviewer-ui.description: Exception route for UI-facing implementation review. Use only when the primary risk is user-visible behavior, accessibility, responsive layout, frontend state, visual regression, or design-system fit. Default to code-reviewer for general implementation correctness and backend review.

code-reviewer-documentation.description: Exception route for documentation review. Use only when the diff is mostly prose, setup flow, operator guidance, terminology, or human-facing documentation. Default to code-reviewer when code correctness is the primary risk, even if docs changed too.

code-reviewer-xhigh-reasoning.description: Escalation-only xhigh-reasoning code reviewer. Use only after default code-reviewer when there is a named high-risk concern: security boundary, data loss, concurrency race, migration correctness, or public API breakage. Default to code-reviewer for routine diffs, style, tests, docs, UI, and general correctness review. Reviews the diff against the task; does not redesign the approach.

approach-advisor-xhigh-reasoning.description: Escalation-only xhigh-reasoning approach advisor. Use only when the operator or planner has a named strategic decision that would materially change architecture, persistence, deployment, or integration boundaries. Do not use for normal planning, implementation review, code review, or vague uncertainty. Advisory only; does not approve plans, review diffs, or implement.

scout-researcher-capable.description: Exception route for research that needs synthesis beyond the default scout. Use only for unfamiliar code paths, cross-repo or multi-domain investigation, ambiguous root-cause discovery, external docs plus local code, or questions without a known file target. Default to scout-researcher for normal bounded discovery. Read-only; do not use for edits.
